rationale: The cited paper is used in the methods section to support identification choices, not just as general background. The citing study explicitly invokes Shi, Grewal, and Sridhar (2021) to justify that firms mimic and learn disclosure behavior from peers, which underpins the relevance of its peer-based instruments for selective disclosure. It also cites that work to argue why broader sector peers are less relevant than close industry peers, supporting the exclusion logic of its first-stage correction.
relation_description: The citing paper applies insights from the cited herding-in-disclosure study to construct and defend peer-based instruments in its endogeneity and sample-selection corrections. It transfers the cited paper's peer disclosure logic to a different empirical question (governance mode choice).
